title: quercus robur var. erucifolia
intro: Quercus robur var. erucifolia, also known as the common oak with curled leaves, is a variety of the pedunculate oak tree (Quercus robur) native to Europe. The leaves of this plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including skin problems and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The medicinal properties of Quercus robur var. erucifolia are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, from skin irritations to gastrointestinal issues.
